By the dawn,
and the ten nights,
and the even and the odd,
and by the night when it departs.
Is there in this an oath for one endowed with understanding?Footnote-1
Have youFootnote-2 not seen how your Lord dealt with Ad
of Iram,Footnote-3 known for their lofty columns,
the like of whom no nation was ever created in the lands of the world?Footnote-4
And how did He deal with Thamud who hewed out rocks in the valley?Footnote-5
And with Pharaoh of the tent pegsFootnote-6
who transgressed in the countries of the world
spreading in them much corruption?
Then their Lord unloosed upon them the lash of chastisement.
Truly your Lord is ever watchful.Footnote-7
As for man,Footnote-8 when his Lord tests him by exalting him and bestowing His bounties upon him, he says: “My Lord has exalted me.”
But when He tests him by straitening his sustenance, he says: “My Lord has humiliated me.”Footnote-9
But no;Footnote-10 you do not treat the orphan honourably,Footnote-11
and do not urge one another to feed the poor,Footnote-12
and greedily devour the entire inheritance,Footnote-13
and love the riches, loving them ardently.Footnote-14
But no;Footnote-15 when the earth is ground to powder,
and when your Lord appearsFootnote-16 with rows upon rows of angels,
and when Hell is brought near that Day. On that Day will man understand, but of what avail will that understanding be?Footnote-17
He will say: “Would that I had sent ahead what would be of avail for this life of mine!”
Then on that Day Allah will chastise as none other can chastise;
and Allah will bind as none other can bind.
(On the other hand it will be said): “O serene soul!Footnote-18
Return to your LordFootnote-19 well-pleased (with your blissful destination), well-pleasing (to your Lord).
So enter among My (righteous) servants
and enter My Paradise.”
